title: echinocereus engelmannii subsp. howei
intro: Echinocereus engelmannii subsp. howei, also known as Engelmann's hedgehog cactus or Howei's hedgehog cactus, is a rare and critically endangered species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and potentially treat various skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and benefits.
